interviewStages: Behavioral: ownership, taste, customer empathy for film/creative professionals
codingTopics: NLP and pipelines: tokenization, chunking, retrieval over documents
Streaming: token streams and batched inference
Search and ranking: vector plus keyword retrieval, top-K with heaps
Data structures: tries, inverted indexes, LRU caches
systemDesignTopics: Design a video generation pipeline that runs at multiple quality tiers
Design a credit / billing system for variable-cost generation jobs
Design a creative editor that integrates generative with classic editing

coding: At Runway, given a stream of model inference requests, batch them to maximize GPU utilization while keeping per-request latency under a bound. Discuss the queue and complexity.
Chunk a long document into overlapping passages for retrieval, respecting sentence boundaries. Discuss the sliding-window approach.
Given a query and an inverted index over embeddings, return the top-K most similar items using a heap. Discuss ranking.
Deduplicate near-identical generated outputs using shingling and hashing. Walk through complexity.
Implement an LRU cache for expensive model inference requests keyed by a prompt hash with TTL eviction. Discuss the data structure.
behavioral: Tell me about a project you are proud of at Runway scale and the hardest tradeoff you made.
Give me an example that shows ownership, taste, customer empathy for film/creative professionals.
Describe a time you disagreed with a teammate or manager. How did it resolve?
Tell me about a time you took ownership of an ambiguous problem end to end.
systemDesign: Design a video generation pipeline that runs at multiple quality tiers.
Design a credit / billing system for variable-cost generation jobs.
Design a creative editor that integrates generative with classic editing.
values: Runway's mission carries real responsibility. Describe a time you weighed capability against safety.
How do you evaluate an AI feature where a wrong answer has real consequences?
Tell me about a time you were rigorous about data quality or evaluation when it slowed you down.
other: How does Runway compare to Pika or OpenAI Sora?

Runway Interview Guide (2026): AI Video Creation Platform

Updated May 6, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Runway
runwayml.com ↗

Runway is the AI video creation platform — known for Gen-3 (text-to-video) and a deep set of creative tools used by film and advertising professionals. Series D in 2024. The interview emphasizes deep generative-video research, creative-tools product engineering, and the unique blend of professional and consumer use cases.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding (Python or TypeScript) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 ML system design or product system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Research candidates get a paper-discussion round. Cycle: 4–5 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a video generation pipeline that runs at multiple quality tiers

- Design a credit / billing system for variable-cost generation jobs

- Design a creative editor that integrates generative with classic editing

- Coding: medium-hard DSA

- Behavioral: ownership, taste, customer empathy for film/creative professionals

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE: $185K–$255K total

- Senior SE: $265K–$365K total

- Staff / ML Research: $380K–$570K+ total

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Python (research / serving) and TypeScript (product)

- Understand video diffusion, motion priors, and temporal coherence

- Brush up on video editing pipelines, NLE concepts, and color science

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Runway remote-friendly?

Hubs in NYC (HQ) and San Francisco. Many engineering roles remote within US/Europe.

How does Runway compare to Pika or OpenAI Sora?

Runway has the strongest professional / film customer base and a deeper editing product. Pika competes on consumer simplicity. Sora is highest-quality but limited access. Runway pays competitively at top of AI startup tier.

What is the engineering culture?

Research-engineering blended; strong art-and-tech identity. Customer-driven by film and advertising professionals.
